TSAN-0036: sys.monitoring: the eval loop reads a code object's active_monitors.tools[] with no lock while _Py_Instrument replaces the whole struct under LOCK_CODE (no_tools_for_local_event vs force_instrument_lock_held)

TSAN-0035: socket: PySocketSockObject.sock_timeout is read and written with plain non-atomic accesses (gettimeout() vs setblocking()), the one per-socket scalar left behind by the gh-128277 free-threading conversion
TSAN-0034: interpreter finalization: handle_thread_shutdown_exception reads interp->threads.head in an assert() before _PyEval_StopTheWorld, racing an exiting thread's tstate_delete_common

TSAN-0031: _elementtree.TreeBuilder: concurrent feed of one shared builder races its unsynchronized parse state (self->data / self->last / self->this / self->last_for_tail / self->index / self->stack); treebuilder_handle_* have no lock / no critical section
TSAN-0030: sys.monitoring: concurrent use_tool_id() races on the interpreter-global tool-name registry (monitoring_use_tool_id_impl)
TSAN-0029: frameobject/sys: a running frame's f_trace is written by trace_trampoline without the frame's critical section, racing the @critical_section f_trace_opcodes/f_trace setters
TSAN-0025: readline: set_auto_history() writes the module-global should_auto_add_history flag without synchronization
TSAN-0018: dict: readers of dk_nentries use plain loads instead of the existing LOAD_KEYS_NENTRIES macro, racing the atomic bump (object.__getstate__, dict.clear, and the public PyDict_Next)
TSAN-0014: list: concurrent list.sort() of a shared list races (binarysort rewrites the detached array with no critical section)
TSAN-0013: list: non-atomic item/size reads race list_resize's atomic publish on a shared list (Py_SIZE / bytes_join / marshal readers)
TSAN-0011: sys: addaudithook lazily creates interp->audit_hooks with no lock, racing should_audit on every audit event
TSAN-0006: itertools: concurrent repr() of a shared count() races count_repr's plain read of lz->cnt against count_next's atomic advance
TSAN-0005: _decimal: concurrent hash() of a shared Decimal races on the cached hash field (dec_hash)
